task: Write a Python function kth_ordered(words, k) returning the k-th word, counting from 1, when the words are ordered by increasing length, and words of equal length are ordered reverse-alphabetically among themselves. Return None when k is out of range. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function correctly implements the required logic for ordering words by increasing length and reverse-alphabetical order among equal-lengthed words, while also handling the specified edge case where k is out of range.
trace:
**
To solve this problem, I first grouped the words by their length. This allows me to handle each group separately. For each group, I sorted the words reverse-alphabetically to meet the specified ordering requirement for words of equal length. After sorting all groups, I flattened the list of lists into a single sorted list. Finally, I returned the k-th word from this sorted list, adjusting for zero-based indexing by using `k-1`. I also included an edge case check at the beginning to return `None` if `k` is out of range.
